# Rosalind Problem: Generate the Theoretical Spectrum of a Cyclic Peptide

## Problem Description
The theoretical spectrum of a cyclic peptide is the multiset of all subpeptide masses of the peptide. For a cyclic peptide of length n, there are n subpeptides of each length from 1 to n, and the total number of subpeptides is n².

## Solution Approach
1. Create a dictionary mapping amino acid symbols to their masses
2. For a cyclic peptide, generate all possible subpeptides by taking contiguous subsequences
3. Calculate the mass of each subpeptide
4. Return the masses in ascending order

## C++ Implementation

```cpp
#include <iostream>
#include <vector>
#include <map>
#include <algorithm>
#include <string>

using namespace std;

vector<int> generateTheoreticalSpectrum(string peptide) {
    // Dictionary mapping amino acid symbols to their masses
    map<char, int> massTable = {
        {'A', 71}, {'C', 103}, {'D', 115}, {'E', 129},
        {'F', 147}, {'G', 57}, {'H', 137}, {'I', 113},
        {'K', 128}, {'L', 113}, {'M', 131}, {'N', 114},
        {'P', 97}, {'Q', 128}, {'R', 156}, {'S', 87},
        {'T', 101}, {'V', 99}, {'W', 186}, {'Y', 163}
    };
    
    int n = peptide.length();
    vector<int> spectrum;
    
    // Add mass 0 for the empty subpeptide
    spectrum.push_back(0);
    
    // Generate all subpeptides of length 1 to n
    for (int length = 1; length <= n; length++) {
        for (int start = 0; start < n; start++) {
            int mass = 0;
            // Calculate mass for subpeptide starting at 'start' with given 'length'
            for (int i = 0; i < length; i++) {
                int position = (start + i) % n;  // Handle cyclic nature
                mass += massTable[peptide[position]];
            }
            spectrum.push_back(mass);
        }
    }
    
    // Sort the spectrum in ascending order
    sort(spectrum.begin(), spectrum.end());
    
    return spectrum;
}

int main() {
    // Read input
    string peptide;
    cin >> peptide;
    
    // Generate theoretical spectrum
    vector<int> result = generateTheoreticalSpectrum(peptide);
    
    // Output result
    for (int i = 0; i < result.size(); i++) {
        cout << result[i];
        if (i < result.size() - 1) {
            cout << " ";
        }
    }
    cout << endl;
    
    return 0;
}
```

## Example
**Input:**
```
LEQN
```

**Output:**
```
0 113 128 186 241 299 357 370 428 485 536 549 607 664 722 735 772 829 842 899 957 1014 1027 1084 1142 1155 1213 1270 1327 1385 1442 1499 1557 1614 1672 1729 1786 1844 1901 1958 2015 2073 2130 2187
